title: Uragoga andamanica
taxonomy: Uragoga andamanica is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to the Andaman and Nicobar Islands, where it can be found growing in the wild. Uragoga andamanica is a member of the genus Uragoga, which contains several other species of plants. The exact classification of Uragoga andamanica is as follows: it is a species of the genus Uragoga, family Apocynaceae, order Gentianales, class Magnoliopsida, and kingdom Plantae. Further classification may vary depending on the source.
uses: Uragoga andamanica is reported to have medicinal uses, with its roots being used in traditional medicine by the indigenous communities of the Andaman and Nicobar Islands.
